data.title: Hyliion Wins $42M Navy Power Generation Contract
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Hyliion Holdings ( (HYLN) ) has shared an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, Hyliion Holdings announced it had secured a cost-plus-fixed-fee contract worth about $42 million from the U.S. Office of Naval Research to support the HELMUR Megawatt Scale Power Generation Units program. Under the deal, Hyliion will design, develop, build, test, and deliver 2-megawatt and 3-megawatt power generation units, with work primarily in Cedar Park, Texas, scheduled through July 2029.

The contract also funds advancement of Hyliion’s core technology, development of alternative core components to improve system capabilities and mitigate magnet supply chain risks, and further expansion of additive manufacturing processes. The award, backed by fiscal 2025 and 2026 Navy research and development appropriations, underscores Hyliion’s growing role in defense-oriented power solutions and provides a multi-year revenue stream that could strengthen its position in the high-efficiency power generation market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HYLN) stock is a Buy
with a $9.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Hyliion Holdings stock,
see the HYLN Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on HYLN Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, HYLN is a Neutral.

The score is held back primarily by weak financial performance (large ongoing losses and persistent free-cash-flow burn) despite a low-debt structure and sizable cash runway. Earnings-call updates add support via clear technical/commercial milestones, accelerating revenue, and cost control, but are tempered by early-stage commercialization and nonbinding agreement risk. Technicals and valuation are secondary factors: indicators are mixed/neutral and a negative P/E with no dividend provides limited valuation support.

To see Spark’s full report on HYLN stock,
click here.

More about Hyliion Holdings

Hyliion Holdings Corp. operates in the advanced power and energy systems sector, focusing on high-efficiency, low-maintenance power generation technologies. The company’s work includes megawatt-scale power solutions and core technology development aimed at enhancing reliability, reducing supply chain risks, and leveraging advanced manufacturing techniques for energy applications.

Average Trading Volume: 4,836,748

Technical Sentiment Signal: Buy

Current Market Cap: $700.8M

Kontoor Brands ( (KTB) ) has issued an announcement.

On July 22, 2026, Kontoor Brands expanded its Board of Directors from six to seven members and appointed former executive Thomas E. (Tom) Waldron as a director, effective immediately, with his term running through the 2027 annual meeting. Waldron, a former Executive Vice President and Chief Operating Officer who previously led the Wrangler and Lee brands and oversaw supply chain and innovation, is expected to bring deep brand and operational expertise as Kontoor works to strengthen its multi-brand platform and pursue long-term growth.

The company also announced that on July 24, 2026, its board declared a regular quarterly cash dividend of $0.53 per share, payable on September 18, 2026, to shareholders of record as of September 8, 2026. The dividend decision underlines Kontoor’s continued capital-return policy, while the board expansion and Waldron’s appointment signal a focus on governance continuity and leveraging experienced leadership to support the firm’s strategic growth ambitions.

The most recent analyst rating on (KTB) stock is a Buy
with a $100.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Kontoor Brands stock,
see the KTB Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on KTB Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, KTB is a Neutral.

The score is driven primarily by solid profitability and cash generation but held back by leverage and the sharp TTM revenue decline. The latest earnings call improves the outlook via stronger margin/EPS trajectory, buybacks, and a clear deleveraging path, while valuation is supportive and technicals are broadly neutral.

To see Spark’s full report on KTB stock,
click here.

More about Kontoor Brands

Kontoor Brands, Inc., listed on the NYSE as KTB, is a portfolio company focused on iconic lifestyle, outdoor and workwear brands, including Wrangler, Lee and Helly Hansen. The company positions itself as a purpose-led organization that leverages a global platform, strategic sourcing and a best-in-class supply chain to drive brand growth and deliver long-term value for stakeholders.

Kontoor operates across apparel categories that span casual lifestyle, performance outdoor and durable workwear, serving consumers worldwide through its multi-brand platform. Its strategy emphasizes scaling these brands globally while maintaining efficient operations and supply chain capabilities to support sustainable, profitable expansion.

On July 24, 2026, Booz Allen Hamilton reported first‑quarter fiscal 2027 results showing revenue of $2.8 billion, down 4.2% year over year, and net income of $198 million, down 26.9%, while adjusted net income rose 17.9% to $217 million and adjusted EBITDA increased 7.4% to $334 million, lifting the adjusted EBITDA margin to 11.9%. The firm highlighted accelerating demand in its National Security portfolio against weaker Civil markets, a $39 billion backlog with a 1.5x book‑to‑bill ratio, sharply higher free cash flow of $261 million, $447 million of capital deployment, a regular dividend of $0.59 per share payable August 28, 2026, and reiterated fiscal 2027 guidance targeting modest revenue growth, roughly 11% adjusted EBITDA margins, and robust free cash flow, underscoring confidence in its strategy of investing in advanced cyber, defense technologies and AI‑native products despite challenging market conditions.

data.title: Bluemount Holdings Names New CFO Following Resignation in July 2026
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Bluemount Holdings Limited Class B ( (BMHL) ) has provided an announcement.

Bluemount Holdings Limited reported a leadership change in its finance function on July 24, 2026, as Chief Financial Officer Ms. Lui Tung Mui resigned for personal reasons, with the company emphasizing that her departure did not stem from disagreements over operations, policies or practices. On the same date, the board appointed veteran finance executive Mr. Chan Ka Ki as the new CFO, signaling a move to maintain continuity and strengthen financial oversight with his extensive background in reporting, budgeting, tax compliance and regulatory risk management.

Mr. Chan, a 47-year-old accounting graduate from Monash University and member of professional accounting bodies in Australia and Hong Kong, previously served as CFO of Incredible Holdings Limited for a decade, overseeing group financial reporting and asset-related transactions. His appointment, which can be terminated by either party with one month’s notice and involves no family ties or related-party dealings with Bluemount, aims to reassure investors and regulators that the company’s financial governance remains stable despite the sudden executive transition.

More about Bluemount Holdings Limited Class B

Bluemount Holdings Limited, headquartered in Hong Kong, is a foreign private issuer listed in the United States and files annual reports on Form 20-F under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934. The company operates through subsidiaries, though the filing focuses primarily on its governance and financial leadership structure rather than detailing its specific industry or core business lines.

data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Banco Santander SA ( (SAN) ) has issued an announcement.

Banco Santander has filed its half-year financial report for January to June 2026, approved by its board on 21 July 2026 and structured around group financial performance, risk, solvency and segment information. The report reflects recent changes in reporting from the first quarter of 2026, which alter the presentation of income statements and management metrics without affecting consolidated profit or balance sheet figures, and restate prior periods for comparability.

During the period, Santander completed the acquisition of TSB Banking Group on 30 April 2026 for about GBP 2.9 billion, fully integrating TSB into its Retail & Commercial Banking business from 1 May 2026, with a 55-basis-point reduction in its June 2026 CET1 capital ratio. TSB adds roughly EUR 53 billion in assets, a largely mortgage-focused EUR 42 billion loan book, EUR 42 billion in customer deposits and a UK branch network serving more than four million customers, strengthening Santander’s UK retail presence while modestly diluting capital.

The most recent analyst rating on (SAN) stock is a Hold
with a $8.50 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Banco Santander SA stock,

The score is primarily held back by financial quality concerns—especially negative operating/free cash flow in 2024–2025 and elevated leverage—despite improving profitability. TSMC ( (TSM) ) has provided an announcement.

In a regulatory report filed on July 24, 2026, TSMC detailed ownership changes among its senior executives for the month of June 2026, noting that Vice President Arthur Chuang reduced his holdings by 1 million shares while fellow vice presidents B.Z. Tien and Lipen Yuan each increased their stakes by 2,000 shares, including related-party holdings. The company also disclosed that there were no new share pledges, capital appropriations, unsecured bond issuances, or share cancellations during the period, while its treasury activity included NT$11.6 billion in fixed-income investments and NT$0.4 billion in dispositions, signaling incremental balance-sheet management rather than major strategic shifts.

These June 2026 disclosures provide investors with transparency into insider shareholding dynamics and TSMC’s conservative capital structure, as the absence of new bond issuance or share cancellations suggests the company continues to rely on internal resources and measured portfolio adjustments. For stakeholders, the modest insider trades and net increase in fixed-income holdings may indicate routine personal portfolio moves by executives and a cautious approach to liquidity and risk management amid ongoing global semiconductor demand.

The most recent analyst rating on (TSM) stock is a Hold
with a $440.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on TSMC stock,
see the TSM Stock Forecast page.

data.title: C.H. Robinson Faces Major Jury Verdict in Texas
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

CH Robinson ( (CHRW) ) has shared an announcement.

On July 23, 2026, a jury in Dallas County, Texas issued an advisory verdict against C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. and two other defendants in a lawsuit stemming from a trucking accident involving an independent motor carrier, awarding $604 million in compensatory damages that could be assessed against the company. The advisory verdict remains subject to post-trial proceedings and potential appeals, and C.H. Robinson has signaled its disagreement with the decision, highlighting the possibility of significant litigation-related costs and financial impacts depending on the final judgment and any appellate outcomes.

The company has cautioned that the ultimate result of post-trial motions, the timing and entry of a final judgment, and any appeals could materially affect its financial condition, operating results, or cash flows. This case underscores the legal and financial exposure logistics intermediaries can face in connection with accidents involving independent carriers, adding uncertainty for investors and other stakeholders as the court process continues.

The most recent analyst rating on (CHRW) stock is a Buy
with a $220.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CH Robinson stock,
see the CHRW Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on CHRW Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, CHRW is a Neutral.

CHRW scores as neutral: resilient profitability and strong cash generation support the financial profile, and the earnings call reinforced confidence in 2026 execution and productivity-driven margin gains. However, the score is held back by a steep revenue downturn, soft near-term technical momentum, and a high P/E with only a modest dividend yield.

To see Spark’s full report on CHRW stock,
click here.

More about CH Robinson

C.H. Robinson Worldwide, Inc. is a global logistics company that provides freight transportation and third-party logistics services, connecting shippers with a network of independent motor carriers and other transportation providers. The company focuses on managing complex supply chains, coordinating truckload and less-than-truckload shipments, and offering related logistics solutions for a broad range of industries.

Jazz Pharmaceuticals ( (JAZZ) ) has issued an update.

At its July 23, 2026 annual general meeting in Dublin, Jazz Pharmaceuticals’ shareholders elected three directors—Bruce C. Cozadd, Heather Ann McSharry and Rick E. Winningham—to serve until the 2029 annual meeting, signaling continuity in board leadership. Shareholders also approved, on an advisory basis, the compensation of the company’s named executive officers, reinforcing support for current management’s pay structure.

Investors ratified the appointment of KPMG Dublin as independent auditors for the fiscal year ending December 31, 2026, and authorized the board’s audit committee to set their remuneration, underscoring confidence in the company’s financial oversight. Shareholders further granted the board authority under Irish law to allot and issue ordinary shares, including for cash without pre-emptive rights, enhancing Jazz’s flexibility to raise capital or pursue strategic transactions without needing additional shareholder approvals; a contingency proposal to adjourn the meeting was not needed and was therefore not voted on.

data.description:

Healthy Extracts ( (HYEX) ) just unveiled an update.

On July 17, 2026, Healthy Extracts entered into a Securities Purchase Agreement under which it issued a $258,750 promissory note to LABRYS FUND II, L.P. The note carries a 10% annual interest rate earned upfront, includes a $33,750 original issue discount, and delivered net proceeds of $225,000 to the company, with prepayment prohibited without the holder’s consent.

The note is convertible after 180 days at the lesser of $2.00 per share or 75% of the lowest closing bid price over the prior 15 trading days, potentially leading to equity dilution. Beginning January 18, 2027, Healthy Extracts must make six monthly amortization payments of $36,964.28 in principal and interest, unless portions of the note are converted into common stock, affecting the company’s future cash flows and capital structure.

data.description:

Outlook Therapeutics ( (OTLK) ) has issued an update.

On July 21, 2026, Outlook Therapeutics’ compensation committee granted stock options under its 2024 Equity Incentive Plan to Chief Executive Officer Robert C. Jahr and Chief Financial Officer Lawrence A. Kenyon, covering 100,000 and 210,078 shares, respectively, at an exercise price of $1.4304. These options, which vest on July 21, 2027 contingent on continued service, were awarded in recognition of their efforts in 2025 and 2026 to advance the company’s Biologics License Application for ONS-5010 through U.S. Food and Drug Administration review and appeal processes.

The committee also approved performance-based cash bonus opportunities on July 21, 2026 for Jahr and Kenyon, set at $420,000 and $200,000, that will only be paid if the FDA approves ONS-5010 and issues a favorable decision on the company’s Biologics License Application by July 31, 2026. The structure of these awards underscores Outlook Therapeutics’ focus on securing regulatory approval for ONS-5010 and ties executive compensation closely to this critical milestone for shareholders and the company’s strategic positioning in the ophthalmic biologics market.

The most recent analyst rating on (OTLK) stock is a Hold
with a $1.61 price target.

data.title: Woori Financial Group Posts Strong Q2 2026 Profit Rebound Despite Revenue Decline
data.description:
Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Woori Finance Holdings Co ( (WF) ) has provided an announcement.

Woori Financial Group reported preliminary consolidated results for the quarter ended June 30, 2026, showing revenue of KRW 13.17 trillion, down 13.9% from the first quarter and 3.0% year-on-year, but with operating income rising 62.8% quarter-on-quarter and 18.7% from the second quarter of 2025. Net income grew 60.5% from the prior quarter and 9.2% year-on-year to KRW 1.03 trillion, while profit attributable to equity holders of the parent climbed 66.2% sequentially and 7.4% versus a year earlier, indicating stronger profitability despite softer top-line performance.

Woori Bank, the group’s main banking subsidiary, also reported preliminary consolidated figures for the same period, with quarterly revenue falling about 23.4% from the first quarter and 25.0% year-on-year to KRW 9.19 trillion. Nonetheless, operating income surged 63.1% from the prior quarter, and net income jumped 61.5% quarter-on-quarter even as it declined 8.1% from a year earlier, underscoring improved short-term earnings momentum but mixed year-on-year trends; all figures remain subject to adjustment pending auditor review.

The most recent analyst rating on (WF) stock is a Buy
with a $72.26 price target.

see the WF Stock Forecast page.

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, WF is a Neutral.

Overall score reflects solid-but-mixed fundamentals: profitability and improved recent cash generation are tempered by a sharp TTM revenue decline and elevated leverage. Technicals are currently weak (below key moving averages with negative MACD), offset by attractive valuation (low P/E and strong dividend yield) and a generally constructive earnings call featuring stronger capital levels and shareholder returns despite notable one-offs and cost/credit headwinds.

Woori Financial Group Inc., based in Seoul, is a South Korean financial holding company with Woori Bank as its core wholly owned subsidiary. The group operates across banking and broader financial services, generating revenue from interest income, fees and commissions, dividends, insurance income and gains on financial assets under Korean International Financial Reporting Standards.

data.title: USA Rare Earth Seeks Shareholder Approval for SVRE Merger and Major Equity Issuance
data.description:
New activity is brewing for USA Rare Earth ( (USAR) ). The company has submitted a Form DEFM14A to the SEC, indicating an upcoming shareholder vote. This form gives shareholders all the information they need to develop an informed vote. Read on to learn more about the proposed company action.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

Double your USAR exposure with Tradr's USAX

USA Rare Earth has called an August 28 virtual special meeting for shareholders to vote on issuing 126.85 million new shares to acquire SVRE Holdings and on a possible adjournment to solicit more proxies. Under the agreed cash-and-stock deal, SVRE securityholders will receive $300 million plus stock and end up with about 34.1% of the enlarged company, while existing USAR investors retain roughly 65.9%.

The board unanimously backs the merger, citing creation of a fully integrated mine‑to‑magnet platform and locking in one of the largest non‑Asian heavy rare earth sources via Brazil’s Pela Ema project. The transaction is contingent on shareholder approval and key government consents, including U.S. and DFC financing structures and a long‑term U.S.-aligned offtake, and carries material risks around valuation, dilution, integration, Brazilian political and regulatory exposure and substantial leverage under SVRE’s DFC facility.

The most recent analyst rating on (USAR) stock is a Buy
with a $30.00 price target.
To see the full list of analyst forecasts on USA Rare Earth stock,
see the USAR Stock Forecast page.

Spark’s Take on USAR Stock

According to Spark, TipRanks’ AI Analyst, USAR is a Neutral.

USAR’s score is held back primarily by deep ongoing losses and weak price momentum (below key moving averages with negative MACD). These are partially offset by a low-leverage balance sheet, recently positive TTM cash flow, and earnings-call signals of significant committed/anticipated funding and defined operational scale-up milestones, albeit with meaningful execution and dilution risk.

To see Spark’s full report on USAR stock,
click here.

More about USA Rare Earth

USA Rare Earth, Inc. is building a fully integrated rare earths and permanent magnet value chain across the U.S., U.K., France and Brazil. Through assets including Less Common Metals, a Stillwater, Oklahoma magnet plant, the Round Top deposit in Texas and, post‑merger, the Pela Ema mine in Brazil, it aims to provide a secure Western supply of materials for aerospace, defense, semiconductors, energy, data centers, mobility, healthcare and industrial sectors.

SVRE Holdings Ltd. operates one of the largest ionic clay rare earth deposits outside Asia via the Serra Verde Pela Ema mine in Brazil, producing all four critical magnetic elements — neodymium, praseodymium, dysprosium and terbium — plus yttrium. Its Phase I mixed rare earth carbonate output is ramping toward about 6,400 tonnes of TREO annually, with a potential Phase II expansion to double run‑of‑mine production by 2030, supported by a long‑term U.S.-backed offtake and DFC project financing.

Average Trading Volume: 15,317,244

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Recent Updates on BSVN stock

Bank7 shares saw supportive analyst commentary over the last day as both Piper Sandler and Keefe Bruyette raised price targets after reviewing Q2 results and the planned Century acquisition. The increases were driven by confidence in Bank7’s strong core net interest margin, robust loan yields, conservative balance sheet, and the expected earnings accretion and geographic expansion from the New Mexico deal.

Analysts also cited the successful exit from oil and gas assets, which simplified the balance sheet and reduced sector risk, as well as stable deposit costs and solid capital that provide capacity for disciplined M&A. While they acknowledged temporary headwinds from elevated IT, remediation, and M&A-related expenses and some execution risk around loan paydowns, they viewed these as near-term issues against a backdrop of resilient core profitability and a compelling use of excess capital in the Century transaction.

Spark’s Take on BSVN Stock
